membership_tiers.what_you_need: Free for everyone — join online then collect, or join in person at any library. Gives you a library card number + PIN.

membership_tiers.what_you_need: Be a full member aged 15+, then do a short one-off induction with staff.
membership_tiers.unlocks: Self-service access 8am-8pm Mon-Sat (even when unstaffed) at 11 branches: Bromsgrove, Droitwich, Evesham, Kidderminster, Malvern, Pershore, Redditch, Rubery, St John's, Stourport, Tenbury.

services.title: Great news for Malvern’s library members as Libraries Unlocked launches next week
services.summary: Libraries Unlocked, the new service will go-live at Malvern Library on Monday 19 August 2024.
what_you_need: Libraries Unlocked is a new service which provides more flexibility for customers and community groups to use their local library at times that are convenient to them, between 8am and 8pm Monday to Saturday.
“Across Worcestershire, libraries are at the heart of community life, and I am glad that we are developing new approaches to accommodate residents' busy lives.
" Libraries Unlocked membership is for ages 15 and over.
Library members who wish to upgrade to Libraries Unlocked membership are required to complete a short induction.
To arrange a Libraries Unlocked induction visit Malvern Library and speak to a member of library staff or call 01905 822722 to make an appointment.

services.title: Hop into Spring with Easter-themed activities at your local library!
services.summary: Looking for some egg-citing family fun this Easter? Worcestershire’s Libraries has you covered with a variety of cracking FREE activities for both the young and young at heart.

services.title: Improve Your Digital Skills at your Local Library
services.summary: Do you know someone with little or no experience using a computer? Worcestershire County Council’s Library service is here to help them build digital skills during Get Online Week.
what_you_need: We are committed to ensuring our local communities have the skills and confidence to access digital information and services safely and open new opportunities for personal and professional growth.
All our libraries provide free access to digital equipment, including desktop computers and printing facilities making them the perfect support centres for anyone needing digital assistance.

services.title: Libraries
services.summary: Discover an extensive collection of books and resources and a wealth of services and activities that encourage reading, learning and aspiration, improve skills and confidence and promote wellbeing and independence.

services.title: Libraries Unlocked
services.summary: A libraries unlocked membership allows customers to enter specific libraries outside of core staffed opening hours.
what_you_need: New technology allows customers with Libraries Unlocked membership to enter the library using their library card outside core staffed opening hours and to use library services independently when staff are not present.
Apply for a Libraries Unlocked membership Libraries Unlocked is live in Bromsgrove, Droitwich, Evesham, Kidderminster, Malvern, Pershore, Redditch, Rubery, St John's, Stourport and Tenbury Libraries.
how_to: visit one of the Libraries Unlocked sites during their core staffed hours - please check the opening times using the links below on our website before planning your visit
complete a brief induction with library staff - they'll guide you through the process and explain the operational procedures

services.title: Libraries Unlocked Launches Next Week at Redditch Library!
services.summary: People in Redditch will soon have more freedom to use their local library at a time to suit them.
what_you_need: To continue enjoying full access to library services, including use of the building during extended hours, customers will need to upgrade their membership by completing a short induction.
“Providing greater access to libraries gives residents more freedom to use them in ways that suit their lives, and the initiative has already had a fantastic impact in other parts of the county.
”To arrange an induction, please visit Redditch Library and speak to a member of staff, or call 01905 822722 to make an appointment.

Customers have described Libraries Unlocked as: “A brilliant idea — it’s enabled us as a family to access the library more,” and “The best thing Worcestershire Libraries have done since the introduction of self-service.

Remove all email permissions from your account - this option will stop all emails.

services.title: Join the library
services.summary: Library membership is free for everyone; join digitally now or visit your local library for full membership.
what_you_need: Become a library member The range of different library membership options are described below.

Digital library membership Worcestershire library members have free access to a wide range of digital resources including eBooks, eAudiobooks, eMagazines and subscription only E-resources, completely free of charge.
Instant membership gives you access to library computers and Wi-Fi for free and allows you to borrow 2 books.
All library members who are 15 and over are eligible to upgrade to free Libraries Unlocked membership.
Libraries Unlocked membership Libraries service behaviour guidelines and byelaws Worcestershire Libraries provides a safe and welcoming space.

services.title: Library Service at Home
services.summary: A volunteer run service that helps to ensure that everyone can continue to enjoy reading from the comfort of their own home.

services.title: Login to my library account
services.summary: Login to your library account to renew your books, reserve books, cancel reservations and change your PIN.
what_you_need: How to login To log into your account: you will need your PIN (personal identification number) in order to renew your books, log into your account or borrow eBooks.

If you want to sign up for, or no longer want to receive marketing e-mails from Worcestershire Libraries you can subscribe or unsubscribe by changing the marketing preferences in your library account.

services.title: Mobile library
services.summary: The mobile library visits 160 villages in Worcestershire every four to five weeks. It brings library services to customers who may be unable to travel to a static library.

services.title: Pay fees and charges
services.summary: Borrowing library books is free, but if you keep the books past their due date, late fees will apply. A full list of fees and charges can be found here.
what_you_need: Replacement library card charges If you have lost your library card, please visit a Worcestershire library and let a member of staff know and they can give you a replacement card.

services.title: Printing and photocopying services
services.summary: Worcestershire’s libraries offer convenient, self-service printing and photocopying at all our locations.
what_you_need: Libraries photocopying and printing service is a low-cost, accessible option for everyday printing or photocopying, whether you need a single page or a larger document.
Print Your Way is available to anyone with full library membership.
If you’re not yet a library member or you have a digital library membership only, it is easy to sign up for full library membership by visiting your local library and speaking to library staff.
How to use the Print Your Way service 1.
Make sure you’re a full library member If you’re not already a full library member, visit a library to sign up for full membership with library staff.

services.title: Renew a loan
services.summary: Renew a loan online to extend your loan period.

services.title: Reserve your library books
services.summary: Items can be reserved online via the library catalogue.

services.title: Your library membership
services.summary: Join the library, sign into your account, search the catalogue, renew and reserve books, pay fees and charges and book a computer.
what_you_need: Emergency Road Closures in Worcestershire Your library membership Your library membership Join the library, sign into your account, search the catalogue, renew and reserve books, pay fees and charges and book a computer.
Join the library Library membership is free for everyone, join digitally now or visit your local library for full membership.
Login to your account Login to your library account to renew or reserve books, update your account details or sign up to receive email updates on library events and activities.
The strategy shapes how library services are evolving to meet the changing needs of residents over the next five years.

services.title: 'Read & Unwind Time' at your local library
services.summary: A time to set aside for the pleasure of reading.
what_you_need: It’s easy to join in.
At Libraries Unlocked locations you will need to have upgraded to Libraries Unlocked membership to enjoy Read and Unwind Time outside core staffing hours.
If you haven’t already done so, please arrange a Libraries Unlocked induction and free membership upgrade with local library staff before attending your first Read & Unwind Time.

services.title: Ask for a Book
services.summary: Ask for a Book is new online personalised book recommendation service offering reading suggestions which can be collected from the reader’s local library.
what_you_need: Any adult can sign-up to join the service, and request a curated selection of up to three books to be chosen specifically for them.
Register on the Ask for a Book website to discover what recommendations we have in store for you.

services.title: Read and discover
services.summary: Develop a love of reading, imagination and discovery and improving literacy through seasonal challenges, themed collections and competitions.

services.title: Reading well
services.summary: Reading Well is a dedicated collection of books that provides information and support for managing mental health and wellbeing for children, young people and adults.
what_you_need: Reading Well books are available to borrow from all Worcestershire libraries.

services.title: Worcestershire Reading Groups Recommend
services.summary: Recommended Reads by Worcestershire book groups.
what_you_need: All titles are widely available through libraries across Worcestershire, making it easy for groups to access and enjoy them together.

services.title: Aged 13–24? Volunteer with Worcestershire’s Libraries this summer
services.summary: Libraries in Worcestershire are on the lookout for enthusiastic young people aged 13 to 24 to volunteer during this year’s Summer Reading Challenge, as part of celebrations for the National Year of Reading 2026
what_you_need: To sign up online, please visit the Summer Reading Challenge webpage.

services.title: Celebrate World Book Day at your local library
services.summary: Libraries across Worcestershire are inviting families to celebrate the magic of reading this World Book Day, as part of the National Year of Reading.
what_you_need: For children aged 4 to 9 years.

services.title: Free family fun this summer with Worcestershire Libraries & National Trust Croome
services.summary: This summer, Worcestershire’s libraries and National Trust Croome are teaming up to offer families an unforgettable experience of reading, creativity, and outdoor play, with an exciting programme of Summer Reading Challenge events and the National Trust’s Summer of Play.
what_you_need: All Summer Reading Challenge sessions and events are free to attend.

services.title: Get involved with the National Year of Reading 2026
services.summary: Through stories, events and welcoming spaces, Worcestershire libraries help people of all ages discover, enjoy and share a love of books.

services.title: Get school ready with Worcestershire's libraries
services.summary: Is your little one starting school in September? Are you wondering if they are ready? Worcestershire's libraries is here to support the journey!

services.title: Improve Your Digital Skills at Worcestershire Libraries This Summer!
services.summary: Worcestershire County Council’s Library service is excited to announce new Learn My Way introduction sessions and support for people of all ages who are looking to improve their digital skills.
what_you_need: We are committed to ensuring our local communities have the skills and confidence to access digital information and services safely.
Anyone seeking support to get started with Learn My Way can simply visit their local library and ask the staff for help with signing up.

services.title: Libraries ‘Go All In’ for the National Year of Reading 2026
services.summary: Libraries bring people together. Through stories, events and welcoming spaces, local libraries support people of all ages to discover, enjoy and share a love of reading.
what_you_need: In addition, libraries are one of the few places you can visit without any cost, as membership is FREE for everyone who lives in Worcestershire.

services.title: Library Christmas & New Year Opening Times
services.summary: Opening Times for Worcestershire Libraries across the festive period.

services.title: Library Christmas and New Year opening times
services.summary: Christmas and New Year Opening Times have been shared by Worcestershire Libraries.

services.title: Summer Reading Challenge
services.summary: The Summer Reading Challenge is the UK’s biggest free reading for pleasure programme for children.

services.title: Young Poet Laureate
services.summary: Worcestershire Young Poet Laureate is run jointly by Severn Arts and Worcestershire Libraries, celebrating the talented voices of young local poets.

services.title: Connect with others and boost your wellbeing
services.summary: Improve health and wellbeing through opportunities to connect, inform and build confidence and resources that support self-help.
what_you_need: Readers Groups Informal groups of readers, who can connect socially while discussing books that have been borrowed from the Library.
Signposting Library staff are experts in supporting customer to access a range of information, resources and to source additional services that might be of help to them.

services.title: Libraries offer a free warm space to its residents this winter
services.summary: As winter sets in, libraries across Worcestershire are here to provide everyone with a free, warm, and welcoming space.
what_you_need: Everyone is welcome to use the library as a warm space, and the friendly, knowledgeable staff are always on hand to ensure visitors feel comfortable and well-supported.

services.title: Stay warm and connected at your local library this winter
services.summary: As the colder months return, libraries across Worcestershire offer free, warm, and welcoming spaces for everyone in the community. Whether you’re seeking a quiet place to read or study an opportunity to take part in a library group or activity, or support with digital skills, job searching or setting up a business, your local library has something for everyone.

services.title: Warm welcome
services.summary: Worcestershire Libraries are part of the Warm Welcome Network which lists venues that can provide a warm welcome for those struggling to heat their homes this winter.
what_you_need: Libraries offer a wide range of free services to allow Worcestershire residents to stay connected: Welcoming and warm spaces to work, study, read and research or meet with friends.
Library membership is free and library members can borrow books download e-books, audiobooks, e-magazines, e-newspapers and search online reference sources for free.
Worcestershire’s 21 public libraries provide free access to computers, the internet and Wi-Fi and support from Digital Champions to develop IT skills and gain confidence to access online services.
